"'''Fett Club'''" was a 12-page [[Infinities]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] featured in ''[[Star Wars Tales 24]]'' and collected in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 6]]''.

==Plot summary==
The comic is about [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku's]] plans to find a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] for a [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|clone army]], disguised as an advertisement to become a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]]. Fett Club pokes fun at [[Wikipedia:Fight Club (novel)|''Fight Club'']], [[Wikipedia:Monty Python and the Holy Grail|''Monty Python and the Holy Grail'']], [[fanboys]], and [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] among others.

===The Rules of Fett Club===
[[File:FettClubpic--Ewok.jpg|thumb|250px|Some odd Mandalorian prospects.]]

*The first rule of Fett Club: you do not talk about Fett Club.
*The second rule of Fett Club: you do not talk about Fett Club!
*The third rule of Fett Club: raise your hand and wait to be called on. [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] do not give out info needlessly.
*The fourth rule of Fett Club: ignorance is not bliss. It's deadly. So be confident.
*The fifth rule of Fett Club: don't be stupid!
*The sixth rule of Fett Club: know your limitations.
*The seventh rule of Fett Club: no [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]]. Ever!
*The eighth rule of Fett Club: we do not gamble with our money. We invest.
*The ninth rule of Fett Club: when answering an advertisement always read the fine print.

==Continuity==
The name and premise of the comic is a reference to the book [[Wikipedia:Fight Club (novel)|''Fight Club'']].

Fett Club's address is "[[1138 (easter egg)|1138]]." Outside of Fett Club, [[Wikipedia:The Beatles|The Beatles]] can be seen crossing the street in the image as their famous [[Wikipedia:Abbey Road (album)|''Abbey Road'']] album. Also, [[Randy Stradley]], [[Kevin Rubio]] and [[Jeremy Barlow]] can be seen begging on the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] street.

The prospective [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] include a [[Wikipedia:Dalek|Dalek]], a [[Wikipedia:Clown|clown]], and a [[Wikipedia:Big Boy (restaurant)|Bob's Big Boy]]. Other prospective Mandalorians are playing [[Wikipedia:Monopoly (game)|Monopoly]].

The answers "Yellow. No, blue!" and "South African or North European?" are references to [[Wikipedia:Monty Python and the Holy Grail|''Monty Python and the Holy Grail'']]. The last candidate also mentions waiting for three months outside of [[Wikipedia:Grauman's Chinese Theater|Mann's Chinese Theater]].

A Dalek from ''Doctor Who'' appears in the comic. The illustrator, [[Roger Langridge]], has worked on several of the comic stories in the official ''Doctor Who Magazine''.
